content.problemStatement:
Akhil comes across a string **S** of length **N**. He started wondering about the smallest lexicographical subsequence of string **S** of length **K**.

A subsequence of a string is formed by deleting some characters (possibly none) from it's original string.

A string **A** is said to be lexicographically smaller than the string **B** of the same length if at the first position where **A** and **B** differ, **A** contains a letter which appears earlier in the dictionary than the corresponding letter in **B**.
constraints: 1 ≤ |S| ≤ 10^5 (overall sum of lengths across test cases fits in memory)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains the string S (lowercase letters only). The second line contains the integer K (1 ≤ K ≤ |S|).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line – the lexicographically smallest subsequence of S having length exactly K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case the smallest subsequence of length 3 is "abc". For the second test case it is "ab".
All subsequences of length 2 are: ab, ac, ad, ae, bc, bd, be, cd, ce, de. The lexicographically smallest is "ab".
Possible subsequences of length 2: cb, ca, ba. The smallest is "ba".

solution.approach:
We scan the string from left to right while maintaining a stack that stores the currently built subsequence. For each character c at position i we try to pop larger characters from the top of the stack as long as:
1) The top character > c (popping makes the result lexicographically smaller).
2) After popping we still have enough characters left to reach length K (i.e., stackSize‑1 + (N‑i‑1) ≥ K).
After possible pops we push c if the stack size is still less than K. At the end the stack contains the answer (its first K characters). This is O(N) per test case and uses O(K) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N = |S|. Overall O(T·N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) auxiliary space for the stack (worst‑case O(N) if K = N).
